# Sound / Multimodal

| Method Code | Short Code | Canonical Name | Definition / What to try | Mechanism | Minimum Viable Experiment | Primary Metrics |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| AUD-001 | AUD | Audio-On Cue Mode | When audio is expected, overlay only cues and structure, not full language. | Avoids redundancy overload. | Audio-on cue vs full overlay. | Visual recall; gist |
| AUD-002 | MFM | Muted-First Mode | Overlay carries enough meaning without sound. | Supports sound-off viewing. | Muted-first vs cue-mode. | Muted gist; effort |
| AUD-003 | DLC | Dual-Layer Captioning | Full closed captions plus separate semantic overlay. | Preserves accessibility while enabling retention design. | Closed captions only vs dual-layer. | Access rating; gist; clutter |
| AUD-004 | NSS | Non-Speech Sound Cues | Show [laughter], [music rises], [door slam] only when meaningful. | Captions include meaningful audio context. | With/without sound cues. | Comprehension; access rating |
| AUD-005 | PAE | Prosody-Aware Emphasis | Map speaker emphasis/intensity to controlled text weight or motion. | Captures tone without excessive animation. | Prosody-aware vs uniform captions. | Tone recognition; effort |
| AUD-006 | SAE | Silence as Emphasis | Use no overlay during silence or facial reaction, then land a short caption. | Protects emotional beats. | Continuous caption vs silence+payoff. | Engagement; tone recall |
| AUD-007 | ACT | Audio Clarity Trigger | If background music/noise is high, increase caption completeness. | Compensates when speech is hard to hear. | Clean vs noisy audio variants. | Comprehension; effort |
| AUD-008 | RRC | Rhythm-Respecting Captions | Avoid text changes on every music beat unless text is music/lyric content. | Prevents text from becoming percussion. | Beat-synced vs semantic-synced. | Effort; recall |
| AUD-009 | TI | Tone Icons | Use small icons for sarcasm, warning, encouragement only when tone is ambiguous. | Adds affective context without long text. | Tone icon vs none. | Tone accuracy; preference |
| AUD-010 | HCV | Haptic Caption Variant | For AR/wearables, use vibration patterns for emotion or emphasis. | Multimodal cueing for accessibility. | Lab prototype with haptic cues. | Tone recognition; workload |
